rationale: HR Manager with a career spanning HR, civil engineering (Pied Piper — fictional), and sales — no ML, NLP, or information-retrieval skills; top skills are OpenCV, Webpack, and PowerPoint with no relevance to ranking or search systems.

Entirely non-technical career in HR, operations, and graphic design split across IT services (Infosys x2) and a fictional company (Stark Industries), with top skills being Marketing, SEO, and Photoshop at beginner level — zero ML or engineering relevance.

Career confined to customer support and marketing management (Dunder Mifflin — fictional company, Paper Products industry), with no production ML or IR work despite holding a nominal Ph.D in AI; top skills are Accounting and Vue.js at beginner level.

Built production recommendation features at upGrad (collaborative filtering, matrix factorization) and has Elasticsearch listed as an advanced skill, but the top-skill profile is dominated by TTS and Object Detection rather than dense retrieval, and there is no evidence of embedding-based hybrid search, learning-to-rank, or NDCG/MAP evaluation ownership.

Civil engineer whose entire career spans two fictional companies (Initech, Stark Industries), a manufacturing firm, and Infosys IT services — no ML, NLP, or retrieval engineering of any kind; skills are Agile, React, and Six Sigma.

ML Engineer at product companies (Swiggy, Aganitha) with NLP classification pipelines and production recommendation work using collaborative filtering, but only 3.2 years total experience (below the 5-year floor) and no evidence of embedding-based retrieval, vector search infrastructure, or offline ranking evaluation frameworks.

7.3-year full-stack/mobile developer with 4 years at Infosys (IT services) doing Android development — no production ML or IR work; Elasticsearch appears as an intermediate skill but career descriptions show only React/Node.js SaaS work, and AI exposure is entirely self-learner via online courses.

13.7-year career as accountant, customer support, content writer, and sales executive split across Pied Piper (fictional), TCS (IT services), and manufacturing firms — no technical or ML experience whatsoever despite a nominal Ph.D in Computer Engineering.

Content writer covering developer/AI topics and then HR manager — both roles are non-engineering and non-technical; writing about ML does not constitute building ML systems, and skills are AWS (beginner) and Next.js at beginner level with no IR or ranking work.

Career entirely in marketing and operations across IT services firms (Wipro, TCS) and a fictional company (Stark Industries), with no engineering or ML background; top skills are CNN (intermediate, no supporting evidence) and Excel/Photoshop/Tally.
